name: Additive H1 antagonism
actions: Avoid unnecessary combinations of strongly sedating antihistaminic agents.
Use the lowest effective doses and monitor for impairment, falls risk, and oversedation (educational).

rationale: Both drugs antagonize the H1 histamine receptor.
Combined H1 blockade can increase sedation, cognitive slowing, and psychomotor impairment.

explanation_template: {A_name} and {B_name} both antagonize the H1 histamine receptor. Combined use may increase sedation and impairment (educational).

references.citation: PD stacking: additive H1 antagonism increases sedation/impairment risk.
